content: Onboarding wizards: 3-7 steps is optimal. Must-haves: step progress indicator (e.g. 'Step 2 of 4'), back/next navigation without data loss, per-step validation with immediate feedback, skip option for non-critical steps, persistence so users can resume. Conditional logic (skip steps based on prior answers) reduces friction for returning users. Progress bars increase completion rates 30-50%. Key metrics: completion rate, time-to-activation, feature adoption in first week. Pitfall: too many steps causes abandonment; pitfall: no persistence means users restart. Implementation: state machine or step array with a reducer pattern; each step is a component, parent owns completion tracking.
